tasks.title: Venue Asset whitelisted methods delegate to lifecycle.py
tasks.description: Rewrite Venue Asset whitelisted methods as thin entry points that delegate into hamilton_erp/lifecycle.py.
tasks.details:
Plan section: Task 13 (lines 1916-2036). Modifies venue_asset.py.

Reversal note (2026-05-01): Work shipped (pre-PR-workflow direct commit defining 5 whitelisted methods on Venue Asset DocType controller: `assign_to_session`, `mark_vacant`, `mark_clean`, `set_out_of_service`, `return_to_service`), then REVERSED on 2026-04-29 as part of the AI bloat audit cleanup (PR #34 / `chore/audit-cleanup-deletions` — see `docs/audits/ai_bloat_audit_2026-04-29.md` Finding #1). Reason: the methods were 1-line delegators to `lifecycle.*` never called from the JS — the Asset Board calls top-level `hamilton_erp.api.*` directly. Status remains `done` because the task was completed; the subsequent removal was a deliberate cleanup, not a regression. See `hamilton_erp/hamilton_erp/doctype/venue_asset/venue_asset.py:70-78` for the in-code removal note.

subtasks.title: Manual QA: verify tile rendering in browser
subtasks.description: Rebuild Frappe assets, clear cache, start bench, and manually verify that 59 tiles render correctly on the Asset Board page with proper grouping and colors.
subtasks.details: Run: cd ~/frappe-bench-hamilton && bench build --app hamilton_erp && bench --site hamilton-test.localhost clear-cache, then bench start. Open http://hamilton-test.localhost:8000/app/asset-board in browser. Verify: (1) 59 tiles are visible, (2) Rooms section shows 26 tiles grouped by tier with tier labels (Single Standard, Deluxe Single, Glory Hole, Double Deluxe), (3) Lockers section shows 33 tiles in a single grid, (4) All tiles are green (status=Available after seed), (5) Bulk action buttons visible above each zone ('Mark All Dirty Rooms Clean' and 'Mark All Dirty Lockers Clean'), (6) Tile layout is responsive and readable. Take screenshot for documentation if needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual QA checklist: count tiles visually, verify tier grouping labels appear, verify all tiles show green background, verify bulk buttons are visible and labeled correctly, verify layout is clean on tablet-sized viewport

subtasks.title: Verify tile data attributes and accessibility
subtasks.description: Inspect rendered HTML to confirm each tile has correct data attributes (data-asset-name, data-asset-code, data-status) for future event binding.
subtasks.details: Use browser DevTools to inspect at least 5 tiles (2 rooms of different tiers, 3 lockers). Verify each tile div has: (1) data-asset-name with the Venue Asset docname, (2) data-asset-code with the asset's code (H10, H11, L01, etc.), (3) data-status with the current status value, (4) correct CSS class based on status. These data attributes are critical for Task 18 (popover interaction) where click events will need to identify which asset was clicked. Verify tier abbreviations render correctly: STD for Single Standard, DLX for Deluxe Single, GH for Glory Hole, 2DLX for Double Deluxe, and empty for Locker tier.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual inspection in browser DevTools — check at least 5 tiles across both zones to verify all data attributes are populated correctly and tier abbreviations match the expected mapping

subtasks.title: Commit and push tile rendering implementation
subtasks.description: Run all tests to confirm no regressions, then commit the tile rendering work (asset_board.js and asset_board.css) with descriptive message and push to GitHub.
subtasks.details: Run the full test suite using /run-tests to ensure no regressions were introduced (all 7 modules must pass). If all tests pass, commit with message 'feat(asset_board): card-based tile rendering for Rooms + Lockers' and include both asset_board.js and asset_board.css. Push to origin/main. If tests fail, diagnose and fix before committing. Note: asset_board.js and asset_board.css were already modified in Task 16, so this commit captures the completed tile rendering functionality.
subtasks.testStrategy: Pre-commit: run /run-tests and verify all 197+ tests pass with 11 skipped. Post-commit: verify GitHub shows the new commit on main branch with correct files changed

tasks.title: Phase 2: Stale OOS duration visibility on asset board
tasks.description: Add prominent at-a-glance day-count + staleness color coding on Out-of-Service tiles so a manager can spot stale OOS items from across the board without tapping each tile.
tasks.details:
Replaces the dead-code OOS day-counter removed in Finding #2(b) of the AI bloat audit (PR shipping 2026-05-01).

Required behavior:
1. Day-count badge on every OOS tile in the compact board view, rendered at 12-14px font (vs the dead code's 9px), full opacity (vs 0.75), so it's spottable at glance distance from across the room.
2. Color-coded by staleness: yellow for >7 days OOS, red for >14 days. Bonus: subtle pulse animation on red-tier tiles.
3. OOS section header includes a stale summary, e.g. "OUT OF SERVICE (3 — 1 stale >2wk)" so managers see the stale-count without scanning tiles.

Data source: `asset.hamilton_last_status_change` (already in API response — the same field _format_oos_days_ago uses for the expanded-overlay 'N days ago' text). No backend change required; client-side time math + render is sufficient.

Touchpoints:
- hamilton_erp/hamilton_erp/page/asset_board/asset_board.js — _render_tile() for per-tile badge; _render_oos_content() for section-header summary
- hamilton_erp/hamilton_erp/public/css/asset_board.css — new .hamilton-oos-stale-warn / .hamilton-oos-stale-danger classes; .hamilton-section-stale-summary for the header
- Tests: extend test_asset_board_rendering.py to assert the stale-count appears on the section header when at least one tile exceeds the threshold

Trigger: post-Hamilton-launch UX feedback OR confirmed need at Philadelphia/DC/Dallas rollout where higher OOS volume makes the manual scan painful. Not a launch blocker for Hamilton.

Reference: docs/audits/ai_bloat_audit_2026-04-29.md Finding #2 discussion on 2026-05-01 (Chris reviewed the rendering and approved option (b) DELETE for the dead code, while filing this as the proper UX follow-up).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit: assert the stale-count text appears in the section header when at least one OOS tile is >7 days old; assert no count when all OOS tiles are recent. Browser: visually verify the badge sizes and colors at typical front-desk viewing distance.

tasks.title: Cash Drop envelope label print pipeline (Phase 1 BLOCKER)
tasks.description: Build the missing print pipeline that emits an 8-field label on the Brother QL-820NWB when an operator submits a Cash Drop. This is a Phase 1 BLOCKER for Hamilton go-live per R-012 in docs/risk_register.md — without it, the blind cash drop anti-theft design (DEC-005, DEC-021, docs/design/cash_reconciliation_phase3.md §1) is operationally defeated.
tasks.details:
**Why this is a BLOCKER, not deferable:** The blind drop design depends on the operator NEVER handwriting a number on the envelope. Handwriting can be doctored; pre-printed labels with the system's record cannot. Without the print pipeline, the only options are (a) operator handwrites the declared amount (breaks anti-tampering invariant) or (b) operator drops unlabeled envelopes (forensics exercise at reconciliation). Either defeats DEC-005. Hamilton cannot launch Phase 1 with this gap.

**Canonical spec for label content** — `docs/hamilton_erp_build_specification.md` §7.4 step 4. The 8 fields:
1. Venue name
2. Date
3. Operator name
4. Shift identifier
5. Drop type (Mid-Shift / End-of-Shift)
6. Sequential drop number for the shift
7. Declared amount
8. Timestamp

**Label size** — DK-1209 62×100mm shipping label OR DK-2205 continuous tape (per `docs/design/pos_hardware_spec.md` §5). The Brother QL-820NWB supports both via cassette swap.

**Required deliverables:**
1. **`Label Template` DocType** (or first-cut equivalent) — fields: name, label_size, layout, data_fields. Holds the cash drop envelope template plus future templates (locker key tag, retail SKU). Aligns with the registry concept in `docs/design/pos_hardware_spec.md` §5 + `docs/design/cash_reconciliation_phase3.md` §6 'Open' note on label layout.
2. **Cash Drop `on_submit` hook** — renders the template with the 8 spec fields populated from the Cash Drop record (joining `User.full_name` for operator, resolving venue name from `frappe.conf.hamilton_company` or per-venue config) and dispatches to the printer at `Hamilton Settings.printer_ip_address`.
3. **Brother QL-820NWB printer integration** — preferred path: P-touch Template engine with variable substitution per `docs/research/label_printer_evaluation_2026_05.md` recommendation (templates designed in P-touch Editor, transferred to printer once, then triggered from server with data). First-cut fallback: server-rendered raster image dispatched via the Brother iOS SDK or AirPrint. Whichever is faster to ship for Phase 1 — P-touch Template can come in a Phase 2 follow-up if first-cut is raster.
4. **Hamilton Settings entry** — `printer_cash_drop_template_name` (or rename `printer_label_template_name` to be a 1:1 link to a Label Template record).
5. **Tests** — assert label content includes all 8 spec fields with correct values from a fixture Cash Drop record; assert print is attempted on submit; assert the Cash Drop submit fails (raises `frappe.throw`) if the print pipeline returns an error, so an operator cannot drop an unlabeled envelope.
6. **Operator UX requirement** — if print fails (printer offline, paper out, network error), the Cash Drop submit MUST fail with a clear error message. Operator cannot proceed without a label. This is the structural defense against the 'operator drops unlabeled envelope as fallback' failure mode.

**References:**
- R-012 in `docs/risk_register.md` (this risk's canonical entry)
- `docs/design/cash_reconciliation_phase3.md` (PR #108) — design intent context
- `docs/research/label_printer_evaluation_2026_05.md` — printer SDK + template-engine analysis (Brother QL-820NWB recommended; P-touch Template engine path documented)
- `docs/design/pos_hardware_spec.md` §5 — Label Template registry concept + label sizes
- DEC-005 (Blind Cash Drop) and DEC-011 (Brother QL-820NWB label printer) — see `docs/review_package.md` lines 94 + 112
- DEC-021, DEC-039, DEC-046 — supporting blind cash control + Hamilton Settings printer fields

**Phase placement:** Phase 1 BLOCKER. Must ship BEFORE Hamilton go-live. This task supersedes any 'deferred to Phase 2' framing the print pipeline may have had elsewhere — risk register R-012 promotes it to launch-critical.

**Recommended model:** Opus (touches the blind-cash-control invariant; getting this wrong corrupts the anti-theft design).
tasks.testStrategy:
Unit tests:
- TestCashDropLabelPrint::test_8_fields_populated — submit a Cash Drop, assert the rendered template includes all 8 spec fields with correct values from the record
- TestCashDropLabelPrint::test_submit_fails_on_print_error — mock the print endpoint to raise; assert Cash Drop submit raises frappe.throw with clear operator-facing message
- TestCashDropLabelPrint::test_template_lookup_by_settings — assert the print path looks up `Hamilton Settings.printer_cash_drop_template_name` and uses that template
- TestCashDropLabelPrint::test_no_handwriting_required — assert the printed label content fully covers the manager's reconciliation needs (all 8 fields are present and legible at the spec'd font size)

Integration test:
- Submit a Cash Drop on hamilton-unit-test.localhost; verify the print endpoint is called; verify the rendered label content matches expectation; verify a fake printer-down condition fails the submit

Browser test (Phase 1 go-live verification):
- Operator role: type declared amount, click Submit; observe label printing on the actual Brother QL-820NWB; verify all 8 fields are legible; verify operator cannot complete submit if printer is offline (clear error, no orphaned record)
- Manager role: reconciliation lists Cash Drop records by drop_number and shows operator_declared inline so manager can match a physical labeled envelope to its system record without reading the operator's handwriting (because the operator never handwrote anything)

tasks.title: POS business process gap analysis (research-driven)
tasks.description: Two-phase research + audit task to surface every POS business process Hamilton ERP hasn't yet handled. Phase A is wide-net field research from ERPNext community sources to expand Chris's 17-process starter list with real-world gotchas. Phase B audits each surfaced process against four sources (build spec / Hamilton DocTypes / native ERPNext / ERPNext apps) and produces design intent docs for the top 5 highest-priority gaps.
tasks.details:
**Phase A — Research (Sonnet, extensive web research)**

Search the following sources for forgotten business processes, edge cases that broke deployments, post-mortems, 'we wish we'd known' stories, and gotchas around POS specifically:
- discuss.erpnext.com (ERPNext community forum)
- frappe forums + frappe GitHub issues (frappe/frappe + frappe/erpnext + frappe/payments)
- Reddit: r/ERPNext, r/POS, r/smallbusiness, r/Accounting
- Blog posts and post-mortems from ERPNext consulting firms
- ERPNext app marketplace and frappe.io community apps for evidence of which processes have community-built solutions vs need custom builds

Particular focus areas: cash handling, refunds, voids, comps, multi-tablet sync, end-of-shift, payroll/tip flows, tax remittance, chargebacks, membership lifecycle, inventory shrinkage, manager overrides.

Deliverable: docs/research/erpnext_pos_business_process_gotchas.md — comprehensive list with citation/source links, ranked by frequency of occurrence in field reports. For each gotcha, include: source URL, deployment context (industry / size), the specific failure mode, and what the resolution was (or wasn't).

**Phase B — Audit (Opus, multi-source cross-check)**

Use Phase A's expanded list AND Chris's 17-process starter list as the input set. For each process, check FOUR sources:
1. docs/hamilton_erp_build_specification.md — is the process specced?
2. existing DocTypes in hamilton_erp/hamilton_erp/doctype/ — is there an implementation?
3. native ERPNext — does ERPNext provide it built-in (POS Closing Entry, Pricing Rules, Sales Return, etc)?
4. ERPNext apps / modules (community or commercial) — is there a battle-tested third-party solution (HRMS for payroll, etc)?

For each process, classify the gap as: tweak ERPNext / extend custom / build new / already-handled.

Starter 17-process list (will expand based on Phase A findings):
- Refunds (cash and card) — settlement reconciliation pairing
- Comps and discounts (workflow, not just logging) — manager approval flow
- Voids (mid-shift transaction undo)
- End-of-shift handover between operators
- Customer disputes / chargebacks — documentation + dispute response packets
- Daily / weekly closeout and bank deposit
- Petty cash / supply purchases from drawer
- Manager overrides (price, comp, return, refund)
- Lost / found items
- Staff sales / employee discount
- Membership lifecycle (signup, renewal, cancel, refund) — DC priority
- Inventory / receiving
- Daily safe count
- Bank deposit reconciliation
- Sales tax remittance — multi-jurisdiction (Ontario HST, PA, DC, TX)
- Payroll integration (especially tips paid from till)
- Promotions / pricing tiers (happy hour, member rates, time-of-day)

**Deliverables:**
1. docs/research/erpnext_pos_business_process_gotchas.md — Phase A research output
2. docs/audits/pos_business_process_gap_audit_2026-NN-NN.md — Phase B audit table (markdown table with 4-column status)
3. docs/design/<process_name>_phase2.md (or _phase3.md) — design intent docs for top 5 highest-priority gaps, structured like docs/design/cash_reconciliation_phase3.md (committed PR #108 on 2026-05-01)
4. Flag every process that depends on multi-venue feature flags (anvil_membership_enabled, anvil_tax_mode, anvil_currency, etc) as Phase 2 work.

**Reference model:** docs/design/cash_reconciliation_phase3.md — design intent doc structure to replicate for the top-5 gaps. Reference DEC entries where applicable. The top-5 selection criteria: (a) frequency of mention in Phase A field reports, (b) blast radius if missed at launch, (c) Hamilton-now vs DC/Philly-later phase fit.

**Why this task is research-heavy first:** Chris's starter list is opinion-driven; ERPNext community + Reddit are evidence-driven. Surfacing the gotchas BEFORE the audit prevents the audit from missing entire process categories that haven't crossed Chris's mind. The research phase output is independently useful even if Phase B reshapes priorities.

**Phase placement:** Phase 1.5 / Phase 2 — NOT Phase 1. Hamilton's launch can ship without all 17+ processes solved; this task's purpose is to inventory the gap so phase planning can be deliberate rather than discovering gaps in production.
tasks.testStrategy:
**Phase A success criteria:**
- erpnext_pos_business_process_gotchas.md exists with at least 30 distinct gotchas cited from at least 5 distinct source families (forum / Reddit / blog / GitHub issue / app marketplace)
- Each gotcha has a working source URL
- Gotchas are ranked by frequency, not alphabetically

**Phase B success criteria:**
- Audit table covers every process in the starter 17 plus everything new from Phase A
- 4-column status (build spec / DocType / ERPNext / app) populated for every process
- Top-5 design intent docs each follow the docs/design/cash_reconciliation_phase3.md structure (status header, why-this-doc-exists, numbered sections with reasoning, open/deferred, browser test plan stub, cross-references)
- Multi-venue dependencies are explicitly flagged in the audit table

tasks.title: Cash-side refunds (Phase 1 BLOCKER)
tasks.description: Implement the minimum cash-side refund flow Hamilton needs to ship Phase 1. The build spec has only one sentence on refunds (`docs/hamilton_erp_build_specification.md`); ERPNext's POS refund flow has multiple open bugs (G-019, G-020, G-029 in `docs/research/erpnext_pos_business_process_gotchas.md`); first-refund-night without a documented flow corrupts the audit trail. Hamilton CANNOT ship Phase 1 without this.
tasks.details:
**Audit reference:** Process #1 in `docs/audits/pos_business_process_gap_audit.md`. Classification: build new. Phase 1 BLOCKER (cash side); full design Phase 2.

**Scope at Phase 1 (cash refunds only):**
- Refund DocType OR Sales Invoice Return (use ERPNext's Sales Return + Credit Note pattern, customized for Hamilton's blind-cash invariant per DEC-005)
- Operator UX: when guest requests refund, operator looks up original Sales Invoice → confirms refund amount → cash leaves drawer → system records refund → printed receipt for the guest
- Manager attestation: refund > $X requires manager PIN (uses Manager Override service from Task — see future task once design reviewed)
- Cash drawer impact: refund cash counts against the operator's till; reconciliation accounts for `system_expected = sum_of_cash_sales − sum_of_cash_refunds − tip_pull` (extends the system_expected calculator currently stubbed at 0 — see R-011)
- Receipt printing: refund receipt prints on Epson TM-m30III with refund-specific template (negative amount, 'REFUND' header, original-invoice cross-reference)
- Audit trail: every refund creates an immutable Refund record + a paired Sales Invoice Return + a row in the operator's shift activity log; manager who attested + operator who executed both recorded

**Out of scope at Phase 1 (deferred to Phase 2):**
- Card-side refunds (require Clover Connect API integration — Phase 2 per design intent in cash_reconciliation_phase3.md §5)
- Settlement reconciliation pairing (Phase 2 audit layer)
- Refund disputes / chargeback packet generation
- Multi-step partial refunds spanning multiple originals

**Design intent doc available for context (NOT mandatory spec, awaiting Chris's review):** `docs/design/refunds_phase2.md`. Use as design context if helpful; the canonical Phase 1 spec is the build spec line + this task's scope.

**ERPNext bugs to route around:**
- ERPNext Issues #54183 + #50787 (per R-010): POS Closing fails when same-shift returns are in the batch with deferred stock validation. Mitigation: process refunds outside POS Closing batch; commit each refund's Sales Invoice Return immediately, do NOT batch at close.
- G-002 'deferred stock validation explodes at close': route refunds with `update_stock = 1` at the time of refund, never deferred.

**References:** R-013 (deferred stock validation, see docs/risk_register.md); DEC-005 + DEC-021 (blind cash invariant); design intent in `docs/design/refunds_phase2.md` (review-pending).

**Recommended model:** Opus (touches blind-cash invariant + ERPNext refund-flow bugs).
tasks.testStrategy:
Unit tests:
- TestRefundCashFlow::test_creates_paired_sales_invoice_return — refund creates a SI Return linked to the original invoice
- TestRefundCashFlow::test_updates_system_expected — Cash Reconciliation `system_expected` accounts for refunds (when calculator is wired up at Phase 3, must include refund subtraction)
- TestRefundCashFlow::test_manager_pin_required_above_threshold — refund > $X without manager PIN is rejected
- TestRefundCashFlow::test_audit_trail_complete — both operator + manager IDs recorded
- TestRefundCashFlow::test_immediate_stock_update — refund with update_stock=1 at time of refund (route around G-002)

Integration test:
- Submit a Sales Invoice; submit a refund against it on hamilton-unit-test.localhost; verify receipt printed; verify Cash Drop reconciliation accounts for refunded cash

Browser test:
- Operator flow: lookup invoice → refund amount → cash leaves → receipt prints. Verify audit log captures both operator + manager when above threshold.
- Negative test: refund > threshold without manager PIN must hard-block

tasks.title: Comps manager-PIN gate (Phase 1 BLOCKER)
tasks.description: Add manager-PIN attestation to the existing comp admission flow so Hamilton Operators cannot issue zero-value (comp) Sales Invoices without manager approval. The current Comp Admission Log captures the comp WITHOUT a structural manager gate; this is operationally insufficient and a Phase 1 BLOCKER per the audit (process #2 in `docs/audits/pos_business_process_gap_audit.md`).
tasks.details:
**Audit reference:** Process #2 in audit. Classification: extend custom. Phase 1 BLOCKER.

**What exists today:**
- `Comp Admission Log` DocType captures who issued the comp + reason category + free-text note (per DEC-016)
- `comp_value` field masked from Hamilton Operator (PR #98 closed R-006)
- No structural gate on WHO can submit the zero-value Sales Invoice — operator can issue a comp without manager approval

**What ships in this task:**
- Manager PIN entry on the Comp Admission flow before the zero-value Sales Invoice can submit
- PIN attestation creates an audit record linking: operator (issuing) + manager (attesting) + comp record + Sales Invoice
- Manager PIN stored as a hashed attestation against the User record (do NOT use the actual login PIN — use a separate manager-attestation PIN field)
- Operator UX: enters comp reason + tier → system prompts 'Manager PIN required' → manager taps in PIN → comp submits
- If manager PIN is wrong N times in a row, lock the comp flow on that tablet for M minutes
- Audit trail: PIN attestation immutable post-submit; manager User record gets a derived 'comps attested today' counter for shift summaries

**Out of scope (defer):**
- Manager-override service generalization across refund/void/comp/discount (Phase 2 — separate Manager Override task once design reviewed)
- Biometric or multi-factor manager attestation (over-engineered for Phase 1)
- Manager geo-location / co-presence verification

**Design intent doc available for context:** `docs/design/manager_override_phase2.md` covers the generalized service that this task is a precursor to. Read for context, but the Phase 1 implementation can be specific to comps (a single PIN gate rather than the full override service).

**Recommended model:** Opus (touches blind-cash invariant adjacency + audit trail correctness).
tasks.testStrategy:
Unit tests:
- TestCompManagerPin::test_no_pin_blocks_submit — comp Sales Invoice cannot submit without manager PIN attestation
- TestCompManagerPin::test_correct_pin_allows_submit
- TestCompManagerPin::test_wrong_pin_increments_lockout_counter
- TestCompManagerPin::test_lockout_blocks_after_N_failures
- TestCompManagerPin::test_audit_records_both_user_ids — operator (issuing) + manager (attesting) both in immutable audit log

Browser test:
- Operator role: tries to submit a comp; observes manager PIN modal; cannot bypass
- Manager role: enters PIN → comp submits; audit log shows attestation

tasks.title: Voids — mid-shift transaction undo (Phase 1 BLOCKER)
tasks.description: Build the operator-typo recovery flow that lets a Sales Invoice be voided immediately after submission (within a short window) without going through Frappe's standard cancel-amend (which Hamilton's permissions matrix forbids for Operator role). Phase 1 BLOCKER per audit process #3.
tasks.details:
**Audit reference:** Process #3 in `docs/audits/pos_business_process_gap_audit.md`. Classification: build new. Phase 1 BLOCKER.

**Why a void surface is needed:**
- Operator typo at the cart (rings $40 instead of $4) needs immediate undo path
- Frappe's standard cancel-amend on Sales Invoice is forbidden for Hamilton Operator (per existing permissions matrix — operators cannot cancel submitted documents)
- Without a void surface, operator typo recovery routes through manager intervention via Frappe-desk, which is friction at the cart and forbidden by the permissions design
- Currently: typos are workaround'd via 'submit + immediate refund', which generates two records for one operator error and confuses reconciliation

**What ships:**
- Void DocType (or 'Void Action' on Sales Invoice) with: original invoice link, void reason (Operator Typo / Customer Cancelled / Manager Override / Other), operator who voided, timestamp, void window enforcement
- Void window: operator can void within N minutes of submit (Hamilton Settings field, default 5 min) without manager attestation
- Outside window: void requires manager PIN (uses Task 32 / future Manager Override service)
- Cash drawer impact: void cash returns to drawer (operator opens drawer, returns cash to customer); cash drop reconciliation accounts for voids the same as refunds
- Receipt: VOID receipt prints to overlay/replace the original receipt (or operator gives customer a 'voided' receipt to replace the printed sale receipt)
- Audit: every void creates immutable record; voided Sales Invoice gets a status flag (not actually cancelled in ERPNext sense — Hamilton's void is a logical-only undo with cash-flow correction)

**Out of scope:**
- Card-side voids (Phase 2 — Clover Connect API)
- Voiding submitted POS Closing Entries (different problem; route through manager via Frappe-desk if ever needed)
- Voiding voids (cap at one undo)

**ERPNext gotcha to route around:**
- G-013 (duplicate submissions on slow networks): the void must be idempotent — if operator double-taps Void, only one void record is created.

**Design intent doc available for context:** `docs/design/voids_phase1.md`. Review-pending; use as context.

**Recommended model:** Opus (immutability + audit trail correctness on operator-facing path).
tasks.testStrategy:
Unit tests:
- TestVoid::test_within_window_no_manager — operator can void within 5 min without PIN
- TestVoid::test_outside_window_requires_manager_pin
- TestVoid::test_double_tap_idempotent — two void requests yield one void record
- TestVoid::test_cash_returns_to_drawer_for_recon
- TestVoid::test_voided_invoice_status_flag — voided invoice is logically marked but original record is preserved

Browser test:
- Operator: rings $40 instead of $4 → immediately taps Void → drawer pops → cash returned → void receipt prints. Verify audit log + recon impact

tasks.title: Tip-pull schema present (Phase 1 BLOCKER for schema, full UX Phase 2)
tasks.description: Add the schema pieces (DocType fields + reconciliation calculator hooks) that allow the system to record an operator's card-tip cash pull from the till, so the FIRST tip-pull event at Hamilton does not contaminate blind-cash reconciliation as phantom theft. Phase 1 BLOCKER for schema only — the full operator UX (system-enforced rounding, tip math) is Phase 2.
tasks.details:
**Audit reference:** Process #16 in `docs/audits/pos_business_process_gap_audit.md`. Classification: build new (schema first). Phase 1 BLOCKER for schema.

**The Phase 1 problem:**
- Operators receive card tips that settle T+1/T+2 to the venue's bank, but want cash same-shift
- Operators pull cash from till = tip amount
- Without schema awareness, the till runs short by exactly that amount
- Cash Drop reconciliation flags the short as theft (false-positive)
- This will happen on Hamilton's FIRST shift unless the schema is in place to record + subtract

**Phase 1 scope (schema only):**
- Cash Drop DocType gains: `tip_pull_amount` (Currency), `tip_pull_currency` (Link to Currency, default CAD), `tip_settled_via_processor_amount` (Currency), `tip_pull_difference` (Currency, calculated)
- Cash Reconciliation `system_expected` calculator (currently stubbed at 0 per R-011) gets the hook to subtract `tip_pull_amount` when calculating expected cash. Calculator stays stubbed at 0 for the rest until Phase 3, but the SUBTRACTION is wired so when Phase 3 lands, tip-pull subtraction works.
- A bare-minimum Cash Drop UX: operator types 'tip pull amount' field; no rounding logic, no math, no instructions to operator. Just a numeric capture so the field is populated.

**Phase 2 (NOT this task — separate future task once design reviewed):**
- System-enforced rounding (Canada nickel: round up to $0.05)
- 'Take exactly $X from drawer' instruction UX
- Settlement reconciliation pairing (match tip pull to processor settlement T+1)
- Tax accounting: tip pull as Tips Payable liability, NOT venue revenue
- Multi-jurisdiction rounding rules (Canada nickel vs US penny)

**Design intent doc available for context:** `docs/design/tip_pull_phase2.md`. Review-pending. Captures the full Phase 2 scope; THIS task is just the schema slice that prevents Phase 1 false-flagging.

**Recommended model:** Sonnet (mostly schema additions, conservative; the calculator hook is small).
tasks.testStrategy:
Unit tests:
- TestTipPullSchema::test_field_present_on_cash_drop
- TestTipPullSchema::test_default_value_zero — Cash Drop with no tip pull has tip_pull_amount = 0
- TestTipPullSchema::test_recon_calculator_subtracts — when system_expected calculator runs (Phase 3), tip_pull_amount is subtracted (verify hook is wired even if calculator returns stub 0 today)
- TestTipPullSchema::test_field_masking — operator can write tip_pull_amount; manager can read; existing permlevel rules respected

Browser test:
- Operator: at shift close, types tip pull amount in Cash Drop form; submits; verify recorded
- Manager: views the Cash Drop record; sees tip_pull_amount; reconciliation accounts for it (when Phase 3 ships full calculator)

tasks.title: Post-close orphan-invoice integrity check (Phase 1 BLOCKER)
tasks.description: Build the daily integrity check that detects orphaned Sales Invoices (submitted but not represented in any POS Closing Entry) so a night's revenue cannot become silently invisible to the GL. Phase 1 BLOCKER per audit process #34 — smallest implementation, largest blast radius.
tasks.details:
**Audit reference:** Process #34 in `docs/audits/pos_business_process_gap_audit.md`. Classification: build new. Phase 1 BLOCKER.

**The failure mode (G-023 in `docs/research/erpnext_pos_business_process_gotchas.md`):**
- Sales Invoice submits successfully → operator continues working
- Network blip, browser tab crash, or POS Closing Entry generation hits the deferred-stock-validation bug (G-002 / R-010)
- POS Closing Entry never includes that Sales Invoice in its tally
- Sales Invoice exists in MySQL with valid GL posting, BUT is not paired with a POS Closing Entry
- Manager-side reconciliation matches the POS Closing total to the cash drop count → all looks fine
- Revenue from the orphaned invoice is invisible to the manager's daily review
- Tax remittance under-reports; bank deposit doesn't match expected; only an external auditor catches it months later

**What ships:**
- Daily scheduled job (Frappe scheduler) that runs after EOD: queries Sales Invoices submitted in the past 24h that are NOT linked to any POS Closing Entry; emits an alert (Frappe Notification + email to managers + log entry) listing each orphan with its amount + cashier + timestamp
- 'Orphaned Invoice Report' page in Frappe-desk that managers see at start of next shift; cannot dismiss without resolving (link to closing entry, or manually accept and document why)
- Hamilton Settings field: `orphan_check_alert_threshold_amount` (default $0 — alert on every orphan; can raise to $5 if too noisy)
- Resolution workflow: manager reviews orphan → links it to the matching POS Closing Entry (or creates a new closing entry to absorb it) → documents reason → orphan record marked resolved
- Audit trail: orphan detection records who detected, when, who resolved, when, what action

**Out of scope:**
- Auto-resolution (manager must touch every orphan; the whole point is to surface)
- Real-time monitoring (daily batch is sufficient — orphan invisibility is hours/days, not seconds)
- Cross-day orphan trending (Phase 2 metric)

**Design intent doc available for context:** `docs/design/post_close_integrity_phase1.md`. Review-pending.

**Recommended model:** Sonnet (audit-trail + scheduled-job pattern; small, well-scoped, conservative).

tasks.title: Zero-value comp item verification test (Phase 1 BLOCKER, verify-only)
tasks.description: Verification-only task: write a regression test confirming Hamilton can submit a 100% comp Sales Invoice (zero grand_total) without hitting the ERPNext bug that blocks zero-value invoice submission (G-003 in `docs/research/erpnext_pos_business_process_gotchas.md`). Phase 1 BLOCKER but no new code — just a regression pin.
tasks.details:
**Audit reference:** Process #20 in `docs/audits/pos_business_process_gap_audit.md`. Classification: tweak ERPNext (verify-only — no code change needed if test passes). Phase 1 BLOCKER as a regression pin.

**The risk (G-003):**
- Multiple ERPNext community reports across v13–v16 of zero-value invoices being rejected at submit by various Frappe validators
- Hamilton's comp admission flow generates zero-value invoices (DEC-016 — staff complimentary admission, manager comp, promo)
- If Hamilton's current ERPNext v16 minor version has the bug, comps fail at the till silently or with cryptic error
- Operator workaround: rings $0.01 then refunds; corrupts the audit trail

**What this task delivers:**
- A new test in `hamilton_erp/test_security_audit.py` (or a new module if more appropriate): `TestCompZeroValueInvoice::test_can_submit_zero_grand_total`
- Test creates a Sales Invoice with grand_total = 0 (using a comp item or 100% discount), attempts submit, asserts no exception, asserts the SI is in `docstatus = 1`
- If the test PASSES on Hamilton's current ERPNext v16 minor: regression pin done, Phase 1 BLOCKER closed, no further work
- If the test FAILS: bug is present in our pinned v16 minor; document workaround (force a $0.01 line item that gets refunded, OR upgrade to a v16 minor that fixes the bug) — escalate to Chris before Phase 1 launch

**Why this is a BLOCKER even though it's verify-only:**
- Hamilton can ship Phase 1 only if comps actually work end-to-end at the till
- Without this test pinning the behavior, an ERPNext upgrade (per the monthly cadence in CLAUDE.md) could regress comps without anyone noticing until an operator hits it live

**Recommended model:** Sonnet (test writing — repetitive, mechanical, well-defined per CLAUDE.md model selection rules).
tasks.testStrategy:
**This task IS the test.** Success criteria:
- The test exists in `hamilton_erp/test_security_audit.py` or appropriate module
- The test passes on hamilton-unit-test.localhost against the current ERPNext v16 pinned minor
- The test is added to `.claude/commands/run-tests.md` so it runs as part of `/run-tests`
- If the test FAILS: do not silence — document the failure, escalate to Chris, defer Hamilton launch until the upstream bug is resolved or a robust workaround is in place

tasks.title: Receipt printer integration — Epson TM-m30III (Phase 1 BLOCKER)
tasks.description: Implement the receipt printing pipeline for the Epson TM-m30III (SKU C31CK50021 white / C31CK50022 black, dual LAN+WiFi+USB-C+Bluetooth wireless variant) so Sales Invoices, refunds, voids, and comps print receipts at the till. Phase 1 BLOCKER per audit process #32 — Hamilton cannot serve customers without printed receipts.
tasks.details:
**Audit reference:** Process #32 in `docs/audits/pos_business_process_gap_audit.md`. Classification: extend custom. Phase 1 BLOCKER.

**Existing research/spec (do NOT redo):**
- Recommendation: Epson TM-m30III, SKU C31CK50021/50022 (wireless variant — dual LAN + WiFi + USB-C + Bluetooth in one SKU)
- Replaces incumbent Epson TM-T20III which had split-SKU connectivity (no single SKU had both LAN AND WiFi)
- Per-venue site survey decides WiFi vs wired at deployment time
- Connectivity verified directly against Epson product pages (2026-05-01)

**Files to consult:**
- `docs/research/receipt_printer_evaluation_2026_05.md` — full evaluation, SDK options, comparison table
- `docs/design/pos_hardware_spec.md` §4 — dual LAN+WiFi requirement (post-PR-#104)
- Forthcoming PR — receipt printer SKU correction note (queued in 7-resolution batch from 2026-05-01) clarifying which SKU is recommended; this task may need to re-read that note when it lands

**What ships:**
- Receipt print pipeline triggered on Sales Invoice submit (and refund, void, comp submit per Tasks 31/33/32)
- Epson ePOS-Print SDK integration (iOS-side iPad → printer over LAN/WiFi/USB-C; whichever the venue uses per site survey)
- Print template per receipt type: standard sale, refund, void, comp; each shows venue name, address, GST/HST registration #, items, totals, payment method, last-4 if card, timestamp, drop_number cross-reference
- ESC/POS DK-D drawer kick command on cash sales (drawer pops at end of receipt print)
- Failure handling: if print fails, the SUBMIT does NOT fail (operator can retry print from Sales Invoice form). Unlike R-012 envelope label (which BLOCKS submit), receipt print failure is recoverable — operator hands receipt by hand or reprints; the sale itself is recorded.
- Hamilton Settings: `receipt_printer_ip` (Data), `receipt_printer_enabled` (Check), `receipt_template_default` (Link to Print Format)
- Print Format definitions for the 4 receipt types (standard / refund / void / comp)

**Out of scope:**
- Customer email / SMS receipts (Phase 2)
- Receipt re-print from manager fraud-protection workflow (G-014 in research — Phase 2 audit hardening)
- Multi-currency receipts (Phase 2 — Philadelphia / DC / Dallas)

**Hardware procurement:**
- Hamilton orders ONE printer per `CLAUDE.md` per-venue procurement rule. Confirm site survey result (wired vs WiFi) before order. Per-venue procurement rule prohibits bulk orders across venues.

**Recommended model:** Opus (printer SDK integration + ESC/POS drawer kick + multi-template logic; plenty of edge cases to get right).

tasks.title: Remove hardcoded 'GH Room' tab from asset_board.js (multi-venue refactor)
tasks.description: Replace the hardcoded `gh-room` asset-category tab in `hamilton_erp/hamilton_erp/page/asset_board/asset_board.js:84` with a data-driven approach so Hamilton-specific subtype names don't leak into the multi-venue tab framework. Surfaced as code-vs-spec drift during the 2026-05-01 docs consistency audit (PR #114, F-014). Phase 2 multi-venue work — blocks Philadelphia / DC / Dallas rollout from rendering correctly.
tasks.details:
**The drift:** `asset_board.js` line 84 hardcodes:
```javascript
{ id: "gh-room", label: __("GH Room"), filter: (a) => a.asset_category === "Room" && a.asset_tier === "GH Room" }
```
This adds a 7th asset-category tab (Lockers, Single, Double, **VIP**, **GH Room**, Waitlist, Other) to Hamilton's deployed asset board, contradicting the canonical 6-tab framework documented in `docs/decisions_log.md` §1.1 "Seven-tab structure (fixed order)" and §7 "Category is fixed, subtypes are not."

**Why it matters:** Per decisions_log §7, the 6 asset-category tabs (Lockers/Single/Double/VIP/Waitlist/Other) are the **stable UI taxonomy** that every venue inherits. Subtypes within a category (e.g. Hamilton's "GH Room" within Single, or Philadelphia's hypothetical subtypes within Single) are **venue-specific data**, NOT hardcoded tabs. Hardcoding GH Room as a top-level tab means:
- Philadelphia / DC / Dallas rollouts inherit a Hamilton-specific tab that doesn't apply to them
- Each new venue would need a code change to remove or replace the hardcoded tab
- The data-driven multi-venue model breaks down at the very first non-Hamilton site

**Hamilton works today** because Hamilton actually has GH Room subtype rooms. The bug is latent until the second venue ships.

**What ships:**
1. Remove the hardcoded `gh-room` tab object from `asset_board.js:84`.
2. Confirm Hamilton's GH Room rooms continue to render under the **Single** tab (they have `asset_category = "Room"` and `asset_tier = "GH Room"`; the Single tab filter is `asset_category === "Room" && (asset_tier === "Single Standard" || asset_tier === "Deluxe Single")`).
3. Either (a) widen the Single tab's filter to include `asset_tier === "GH Room"`, OR (b) introduce a tier-aware sub-section within the Single tab that visually groups GH Room tiles together — pick whichever Chris approves visually.
4. Add per-tier subtype rendering within tabs if needed for visual grouping (decisions_log §7: subtypes are venue-specific, rendered from data, not hardcoded).
5. Browser test on Hamilton: confirm all 26 rooms still render in their expected tabs; GH Room rooms appear under Single (or wherever the visual grouping lands).
6. Update or add a regression test in `test_asset_board_rendering.py` that asserts no hardcoded venue-specific subtype names exist in the tab list — only the 6 canonical asset categories + Watch.

**Cross-references:**
- `docs/decisions_log.md` §1.1 — "Seven-tab structure (fixed order)" defining the 6 + Watch canonical taxonomy
- `docs/decisions_log.md` §7 — "Category is fixed, subtypes are not"
- `docs/design/V9.1_RETAIL_AMENDMENT.md` line 12 (post-PR #114) — explicit canonical-spec language: "Hamilton happens to have GH Room as a Single subtype, but Philadelphia / DC / Dallas may have different subtypes within the same 6-tab framework"
- PR #114 commit body — original surfacing of the code-vs-spec drift
- `hamilton_erp/hamilton_erp/page/asset_board/asset_board.js:73-90` — current `get tabs()` implementation

**Phase placement:** Phase 2 (multi-venue rollout). NOT a Phase 1 BLOCKER for Hamilton — Hamilton's deployment works today. Required before Philadelphia / DC / Dallas rollout to avoid each new venue inheriting Hamilton-specific tab hardcoding.

**Recommended model:** Opus (touches asset board UI + visual grouping decisions + multi-venue correctness).
tasks.testStrategy:
**Regression tests (must add):**
- `test_asset_board_rendering.py::test_no_hardcoded_subtype_tabs` — assert that the rendered tab list contains exactly the 6 asset-category tabs (Lockers/Single/Double/VIP/Waitlist/Other) plus Watch and any retail tabs from `site_config.retail_tabs`. The test FAILS if any tab id matches a venue-specific subtype name (e.g. `gh-room`, `glory-hole`).
- `test_asset_board_rendering.py::test_gh_room_rooms_render_under_single` — assert Hamilton's GH Room rooms (asset_category=Room, asset_tier="GH Room") appear under the Single tab.

**Browser test (manual verification):**
- Open Hamilton's asset board in browser; confirm exactly 6 + Watch tabs visible; confirm all GH Room rooms render under Single (or wherever the visual grouping lands).
- Spin up a hypothetical Philadelphia test site (or a hamilton-test.localhost variant with Philadelphia tier names); confirm tabs render the canonical 6 + Watch with NO Hamilton-specific subtypes.

**Multi-venue correctness check:**
- Verify `decisions_log.md` §1.1's 7-tab fixed order is what renders for any venue with the same site_config shape.
